cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what Steve Rowson thinks of the newer football helmets. The options are A, B, C, D. I need to look through the text to find where Steve Rowson is mentioned. Looking back at the passage, there's a section that says: "Steve Rowson is an engineer at Virginia Tech. In his lab, he tests how helmets hold up against different types of hits. No helmet can prevent concussions completely, says Rowson. But he's found that when wearing a newer football helmet, the player's head doesn't gain speed as much after a hit. That lowers the risk of concussions." So the key point here is that the newer helmets reduce the speed gain of the head after a hit, which in turn lowers concussion risk. The options given are about what he thinks. The options are: A. They vary a lot in size. B. They work better than the old ones. C. They slow down the player's running speed. D. They allow the players to move their heads more freely. The passage says the newer helmets make the head not gain speed as much after a hit. The option B says they work better than the old ones. Since the older helmets might not have this effect, and the new ones reduce speed gain, that implies they are better.
